page: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Edsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 1890 GB of local disk capacity alongside Premium Storage compatibility. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2112.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.19 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory subsystem benchmarks generally align with average tier expectations, including a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 15985.3 and a PassMark memory score of 2252.93. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 120.69 GB/sec for read operations and 67.63 GB/sec for write operations. Notably, the hardware achieves top-tier performance in low-parameter language model execution, delivering 373.31 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M model, while medium 7B model prompt processing operates at an average rate of 108.3 tokens per second.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:16 provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. By offering 256.0 GB of RAM on only 16 vCPUs, the instance reduces software licensing costs tied to core counts while maintaining a large memory footprint. However, the 8 physical core limit represents a tradeoff for highly parallelized compute-bound tasks. The combination of high-capacity RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and 1890 GB of local storage makes this server suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, delivering 16 dedicated vCPUs via Hyper-V virtualization. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2112.0 and top-tier text generation speeds of 373.31 tokens per second on small 135M language models. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics sit in the average tier, with large-block memory reads at 120.69 GB/sec. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications by minimizing core-based licensing costs. It is suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v6 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector operations
Strong single-core performance and top-tier small-model LLM text generation
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
